name: Marcus, Leslie
resolved_employer: Kazoo / WorkTango (Austin employee-experience HR software) - Senior Account Executive
evidence: Leslie Marcus is a Senior Account Executive at WorkTango in Austin; RocketReach shows her prior email on the kazoohr.com domain, and Kazoo acquired WorkTango in April 2022, which matches the self-reported 'Kazoo' employer on the Feb-Aug 2022 gifts. Austin location and Technology occupation corroborate.

name: Boyd, Michele
resolved_employer: Benny Boyd Auto Group (Lampasas, TX-based Chrysler/Dodge/Jeep/Ram and used-car dealer group) - sales
evidence: The self-reported employer 'Benny Boyd, Ltd.' resolves to Benny Boyd Auto Group, an auto dealership group founded in 1994 with stores in Lampasas, Copperas Cove, Marble Falls and Lockhart. The employer is identifiable and the 'Sales' occupation fits a dealership, but no public record independently confirms which individual Michele Boyd this is or her role there; her 78701 downtown Austin zip does not match the dealership's Lampasas-area footprint. Her 41 FEC gifts lean Republican (0.36).

name: Harren, Mary
evidence: Occupation and employer were 'requested' (not disclosed) on the filing and no FEC history exists to cross-check. People-search records place a Mary E. Harren at 8308 Navidad Dr, Austin 78735 - matching the donor zip - alongside Steven Harren, and an Austin developer named Steve Harren runs Harren Interests (real estate). That family link comes only from aggregator data and does not establish Mary Harren's own occupation, so no industry is assigned.

name: Ramsey, Lance
resolved_employer: Gjerset & Lorenz, LLP (Austin healthcare law firm) - Partner, government/Medicaid reimbursement
evidence: Lance J. Ramsey is a partner at Gjerset & Lorenz, LLP in Austin, representing public, non-profit and investor-owned hospitals on Medicaid supplemental reimbursement and financing; UT Law J.D. with honors, 1996. Firm is Austin-based, matching the donor's Austin 78731 address and self-reported 'Partner / Gjerset & Lorenz, LLP'. His 28 FEC gifts lean strongly Democratic (0.89).

name: Cassio, Marina
resolved_employer: None reported (self-reported 'Mom' / 'n/a'); likely Marina D. Cassio, California-licensed attorney (environmental and natural resources law)
evidence: Marina D. Cassio (CA Bar #305458, admitted Dec 2015, environmental/natural resources practice) is recorded at 1107 Sanders Dr, Moraga, CA 94556 from Nov 2020 to July 2023 - the same small town and zip as the donor, spanning the May-Aug 2022 gift dates. Moraga's small population makes a same-named second person unlikely, but the donor self-reported 'n/a'/'Mom' rather than attorney, so industry reflects the self-report; she later appears at Stanford as an attorney/PhD student. Why a Moraga resident gave to Austin's Ryan Alter is not established.

name: Chirogianis, Adina
resolved_employer: ADINA Gallery / Austin Dealer in Art (Austin art gallery and dealership) - Owner; former practicing attorney
evidence: Adina Kuper Chirogianis owns ADINA Gallery in Austin, an appointment-only gallery at 1304 Rio Grande St representing emerging and established artists. She is a Texas-licensed attorney since 1999 who left practice for the art world; her State Bar listing remains active. Austin location and self-reported 'Owner / Adina Gallery' match.

name: Stockhoff Flack, Kate
evidence: Public-records aggregators list Kate Flack / Kate Stockhoff as alternate names for Adrienne Kathleen Stockhoff Flack at 1401 Falcon Ledge Dr, Austin, which falls in the donor's 78746 zip. Occupation rests on her own filing ('Stay at Home Mom', employer 'none'); no employment or other public profile was found to add detail.

name: Schurz, Todd
resolved_employer: Schurz Communications, Inc. - Senior Advisor and fifth-generation owner; President/CEO 2007 to Jan 2023
evidence: Todd F. Schurz served as President and CEO of Schurz Communications (a family-owned diversified media/broadband company, originally a newspaper and broadcast group) from July 2007 until Jan 31, 2023, and is now Senior Advisor. His LinkedIn lists him in Austin, Texas, matching the donor's 78703 address and self-reported 'Senior Advisor / Schurz Communications'. Also sits on the boards of 1st Source Corporation and Herschend Enterprises.

name: Hurley, William
resolved_employer: Strangeworks (Austin quantum computing software startup) - Founder and CEO; also founder of Ecliptic Capital
evidence: William 'whurley' Hurley is founder/CEO of Strangeworks in Austin, and previously founded Chaotic Moon Studios (sold to Accenture) and Honest Dollar (sold to Goldman Sachs, where he became a managing director); ex-Apple and IBM Master Inventor. Lives in Austin/West Lake Hills, matching the 78746 address and self-reported 'CEO / Strangeworks'. His 210 FEC gifts lean almost entirely Democratic (0.999); he co-hosted a 2016 DNC fundraiser with will.i.am featuring President Obama.

affiliations.role: Co-founder and CTO (2003-2005); per Wikipedia, Symbiot 'specialized in providing information security for the US government and military, as well as corporate clients.' Historical and indirect - an infosec vendor with government/military customers, not a prime defense contractor.
Co-host, 2016 SXSW fundraiser with President Obama

name: Mitzner, Michael P.
resolved_employer: RIDA Development Corporation (Houston-based real estate development and investment firm) - Vice President, Operations
evidence: Michael Mitzner is VP of Operations at RIDA Development in Houston, previously an Investments Associate at iStar; B.A., University of Pennsylvania. Houston 77010 address and self-reported 'Real Estate / RIDA' match. RIDA is the Mitzner family firm, founded 1975 by Holocaust survivor David Mitzner, with $14B+ in developed assets (Marriott Marquis Houston, Gaylord Rockies, Hilton Orlando). NOTE, not attributed to this donor: RIDA's president/CEO Ira M. Mitzner is listed in AIPAC Houston's leadership, chairs the Yeshiva University board, sits on the American Society for Yad Vashem executive board and the State of Israel Bonds board, and was appointed by Gov. Abbott to the Texas Holocaust, Genocide and Antisemitism Advisory Commission. No pro-Israel or AIPAC role was found for Michael Mitzner himself.

name: Bishop, Ann
resolved_employer: Texas Public Employees Association (TPEA) - Executive Director, 2019 to May 2025
evidence: Ann L. Bishop was Executive Director of TPEA, the membership association advocating for Texas state employees, from 2019 until stepping down at the end of the 2025 session - covering the Oct 2024 gift and matching the self-reported 'Administrator / TPEA'. Career-long Austin public servant: Deputy Comptroller of Public Accounts, first executive director of the Texas Department of Information Resources, executive director of the Employees Retirement System of Texas (2004-2015), and briefly Gov. Rick Perry's chief of staff (2012-13). Her 89 FEC gifts are close to evenly split (0.54).

name: Mooney, Timothy
resolved_employer: Lead Commercial (Austin commercial real estate brokerage) - Founder and broker, SIOR
evidence: Tim Mooney founded Lead Commercial in 2011, an Austin commercial real estate firm doing leasing and tenant/buyer/seller representation; an Austin resident since 1983 who holds the SIOR designation. Austin 78746 address and self-reported 'Commercial Real Estate / Lead Commercial' match. Board member of the Seton Fund and LifeWorks; past president of Entrepreneurs' Organization Austin (2019-20).
